issuesFound.title: Missing `<th>` scope attributes on table headers
issuesFound.explanation: The `<th>` elements in the table headers lack `scope` attributes, which define their relationship to the table rows. Without these attributes, screen readers cannot correctly associate headers with their corresponding data cells, leading to poor navigation and understanding of the table structure. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships).

issuesFound.title: Missing lang attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The HTML document lacks a `lang` attribute, which is required for WCAG 2.2 AA compliance to indicate the primary language of the content. This affects users who rely on language detection for navigation and context.
issuesFound.fix: Add `lang="en"` to the `<html>` tag: "Line 1: `<html lang="en">`"

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on empty <a> elements
Missing accessible name on empty `<a>` elements
issuesFound.explanation: Every `<a>` element without text content or `aria-label`/`title`/`aria-labelledby` lacks an accessible name, blocking keyboard users from understanding their purpose. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Name, Label) and 2.4.4 (Focus Order).
issuesFound.fix: Line 10: ``, Line 11: `Products` → Add visible text or `aria-label` to all `` elements without existing accessible names.

issuesFound.title: Missing `role="button"` on interactive `<div>`/`<span>` elements
issuesFound.explanation: Many `<div>`/`<span>` elements with interactive behaviors (e.g., buttons, dropdown toggles) lack the `role="button"` attribute, preventing keyboard users from identifying them as interactive elements. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Name, Label) and 2.4.7 (Focus Visible).
issuesFound.fix: Line 11: `<button type="button" class="nav-toggle">`, Line 12: `<ul id="nav-menu" role="list">` → Add `role="button"` to interactive `<div>`/`<span>` elements where missing.

issuesFound.title: Missing `aria-label` on empty `` elements
issuesFound.explanation: Several `` elements lack `alt` text or `aria-label`, making them inaccessible to screen readers. This violates WCAG 1.1.1 (Content on Images).
issuesFound.fix: Line 10: `` → Add `alt="Search icon"` or `aria-label="Search icon"`.

issuesFound.title: Missing `role="navigation"` on `<nav>` elements with non-standard structure
issuesFound.explanation: Some `<nav>` elements lack the `role="navigation"` attribute, which is required for screen readers to identify them as navigation areas. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and 2.4.7 (Focus Visible).
issuesFound.fix: Line 11: `<nav id="main-nav">` → Add `role="navigation"`.

issuesFound.title: Missing `aria-label` on `<button>` elements with no visible text
issuesFound.explanation: Some `<button>` elements lack `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`, making them inaccessible to screen readers when their text is hidden or decorative. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Name, Label).
issuesFound.fix: Line 10: `<button type="button" class="nav-toggle">` → Add `aria-label="Menu toggle"`.

issuesFound.title: Missing `role="progressbar"` on `<div>`/`<span>` elements styled as progress bars
issuesFound.explanation: Elements styled as progress bars (e.g., `<div>`/`<span>` with `width: X%`) lack `role="progressbar"`, `aria-valuenow`, `aria-valuemin`, and `aria-valuemax`. This violates WCAG 1.4.13 (Info and Relationships).
issuesFound.fix: Line 10: `<div class="hero-ctas">` → Add `role="group"` and `aria-hidden="true"` if not intended as interactive.

issuesFound.title: Missing `aria-labelledby` on `<button>` elements referencing hidden labels
issuesFound.explanation: Some `<button>` elements use `<span class="sr-only">` for hidden text, but lack `aria-labelledby` to reference the label. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Name, Label).
issuesFound.fix: Line 10: `<a href="/signup" class="btn btn-primary btn-lg">Read more` → Add `aria-labelledby="hero-ctas-label"` to `<a>` elements referencing hidden text.

issuesFound.title: Missing `lang` attribute on `<html>` element
issuesFound.explanation: The `<html>` element lacks a `lang` attribute, which is required for proper language identification. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Language of Page).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: `<html lang="en">`

issuesFound.title: Missing `aria-hidden="true"` on decorative `` elements

issuesFound.title: Invalid autocomplete token format
issuesFound.explanation: The autocomplete attribute value "work email" uses a space separator instead of a hyphen. WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose) requires the purpose of input fields to be programmatically determinable. While space-separated tokens are technically valid in the HTML specification, the specific token "work-email" (hyphenated) is the standard format for work email addresses and ensures consistent recognition across browsers and assistive technologies.
